ops.name: contact-list

params.description: The numeric ID of the account
params.name: account_id

params.description: The page parameter
params.name: page

params.description: The attribute by which list should be sorted
params.name: sort

ops.description:
Merge two contacts into a single contact. The base contact remains and receives all
data from the mergee contact. After the merge, the mergee contact is permanently deleted.

This action is irreversible. All conversations, labels, and custom attributes from the
mergee contact will be moved to the base contact.
ops.name: contact-merge

params.description: The numeric ID of the account
params.name: account_id

input_fields.description: ID of the contact that will remain after the merge and receive all data
input_fields.name: base_contact_id

input_fields.description: ID of the contact that will be merged into the base contact and deleted
input_fields.name: mergee_contact_id

ops.description:
Creating a conversation in chatwoot requires a source id. 

 Learn more about source_id: https://www.chatwoot.com/hc/user-guide/articles/1677839703-how-to-create-an-api-channel-inbox#send-messages-to-the-api-channel
ops.name: new-conversation

params.description: The numeric ID of the account
params.name: account_id

input_fields.description: Lets you specify attributes like browser information
input_fields.name: additional_attributes

input_fields.description: Agent Id for assigning a conversation to an agent
input_fields.name: assignee_id

input_fields.description: Contact Id for which conversation is created
input_fields.name: contact_id

input_fields.description: The object to save custom attributes for conversation, accepts custom attributes key and value
input_fields.name: custom_attributes

input_fields.description:
Id of inbox in which the conversation is created
Allowed Inbox Types: Website, Phone, Api, Email
input_fields.name: inbox_id

input_fields.description: The initial message to be sent to the conversation
input_fields.name: message

input_fields.description: Snoozed until date time
input_fields.name: snoozed_until

input_fields.description: Conversation source id
input_fields.name: source_id

input_fields.description: Specify the conversation whether it's pending, open, closed
input_fields.name: status

input_fields.description: Team Id for assigning a conversation to a team\
input_fields.name: team_id

ops.description: Toggles the priority of conversation
ops.name: toggle-priority-of-a-conversation

params.description: The numeric ID of the account
params.name: account_id

params.description: The numeric ID of the conversation
params.name: conversation_id

input_fields.description: The priority of the conversation
input_fields.name: priority

ops.description:
Toggle the status of a conversation. Pass `status` to explicitly set the
conversation state. Use `snoozed` along with `snoozed_until` to snooze a
conversation until a specific time. If `snoozed_until` is omitted, the
conversation is snoozed until the next reply from the contact. Regardless
of the value provided, snoozed conversations always reopen on the next
reply from the contact.
ops.name: toggle-status-of-a-conversation

params.description: The numeric ID of the account
params.name: account_id

params.description: The numeric ID of the conversation
params.name: conversation_id

input_fields.description: When status is `snoozed`, schedule the reopen time as a Unix timestamp in seconds. If not provided, the conversation is snoozed until the next customer reply. The conversation always reopens when the customer replies.
input_fields.name: snoozed_until

input_fields.description: The status of the conversation
input_fields.name: status

ops.description:
Get conversation counts grouped by channel type and status for a given date range.
Returns statistics for each channel type including open, resolved, pending, snoozed, and total conversation counts.

**Note:** This API endpoint is available only in Chatwoot version 4.10.0 and above. The date range is limited to a maximum of 6 months.
